California Fair Political

428 J Street, Suite 80O
P.O. Box 8O7
Sacramento, CA 98504-0807
Dear John:
I am writing to request written confirmation of your advice to me
during our telephone conversation of October L, L991, regarding
the Rotarian Group Study Exchange Program. As you may recall, the
Van Nuys Rotary CIub has requested that I apply to this program as
their representative. If accepted, I would travel to Argentina
for five weeks to visit its legal, educational, commercial and
historical institutions.
My intent is to learn as much as possible from the leaders of
these bodies about the impact of each on Argentinats political
system, and share with them the past, present and future status of
our own. The program requires that f specifically spend five days
studying the legislative and governmental activities of the Rotary
host district 
itself. 
Upon my return, I will share my experiences
and knowledge with various community groups in the Senatorrs
district, 
and thus carry out the Rotary Foundationts objective of
pronioting international understanding. Enclosed for your
information is a copy of the pamphlet given to me by the Rotary
C1ub.
Rotary j,s classified as a nonprofit charitable organization, which
is exempt from taxation under Section 501(c) (3) of the Internal
Revenue Code. Therefore, if I understand the Politica1 Reform Act
Section 89506, this trip would not be subject to the new gift
Iimit. 
I would appreciate your opinion on this.
Thank you for your assistance in this matter. ff I might add
anything further, please donrt hesitate to contact me.
My best regards.
Sincerely,

sections.question: If I understand the Political Reform Act Section 89506, this trip would not be subject to the new gift limit. I would appreciate your opinion on this.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a five-week international travel opportunity to Argentina sponsored by the Van Nuys Rotary Club (a Section 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization) constitute a gift subject to the Political Reform Act's gift limits?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request from a state senator to FPPC counsel, not an FPPC advice letter response. The letter references prior telephone advice from October 1, 1991, and requests written confirmation. No formal CONCLUSION section present. Document is dated October 3, 1991.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: If I understand the Political Reform Act Section 89506, this trip would not be subject to the new gift limit. I would appreciate your opinion on this.

Box 8O7 Sacramento, CA 98504-0807 Dear John: I am writing to request written confirmation of your advice to me during our telephone conversation of October L, L991, regarding the Rotarian Group Study Exchange Program. As you may recall, the Van Nuys Rotary CIub has requested that I apply to this program as their representative. If accepted, I would travel to Argentina for five weeks to visit its legal, educational, commercial and historical institutions. My intent is to learn as much as possible from the leaders of these bodies about the impact of each on Argentinats political system, and share with them the past, present and future status of our own. The program requires that f specifically spend five days studying the legislative and governmental activities of the Rotary host district itself. Upon my return, I will share my experiences and knowledge with various community groups in the Senatorrs district, and thus carry out the Rotary Foundationts objective of pronioting international understanding. Enclosed for your information is a copy of the pamphlet given to me by the Rotary C1ub. Rotary j,s classified as a nonprofit charitable organization, which is exempt from taxation under Section 501(c) (3) of the Internal Revenue Code. Therefore, if I understand the Politica1 Reform Act Section 89506, this trip would not be subject to the new gift Iimit. I would appreciate your opinion on this. embedding.summary: Senator Alan Robbins, through his Administrative Assistant Miriam Jaffe, requests written confirmation from FPPC counsel regarding whether a Rotary-sponsored Group Study Exchange Program trip to Argentina would be subject to Political Reform Act gift limits, arguing the nonprofit status should exempt it.
